briefing.headline: NATIONAL LIBRARY LARCENY!
briefing.report: A mysterious figure in a red trench coat broke into the National Library of Buenos Aires last night. She bypassed the security grid and stole the original manuscript of Jorge Luis Borges' "Ficciones". A witness saw her fleeing towards a tango salon in La Boca.
briefing.callingCard: A card with a red silhouette. On the back, it says: "FDPLQLWR WDQJR RXWILW"

clues: A restaurant receipt from "El Caminito" in the colorful La Boca neighborhood.
A tango lyric sheet with the words "El Choclo" circled and a note: "Dress code: Tango Dancer."
A metro ticket stub showing a trip ending at Plaza de Mayo, near the pink Casa Rosada presidential palace.

puzzle.title: Tango Cipher Decode
puzzle.description: Decipher Carmen's coded note. The thief left a message encoded with a Caesar cipher (shift left by 3 letters, e.g. D -> A, E -> B, F -> C, etc.). Use the Tango Substitution Guide.
puzzle.question: What does the cipher "FDPLQLWR WDQJR RXWILW" decode to?

buenos-aires.funFact: Buenos Aires has more bookstores per capita than any other city in the world.
buenos-aires.learningObjective: Identify a real Buenos Aires landmark or regional feature from connected cultural and geographic clues.
buenos-aires.accessibilityDescription: Visual evidence for Tango Cipher Decode in Buenos Aires.

briefing.headline: OBELISCO BLUEPRINTS VANISH!
briefing.report: Carmen lifted a set of theater-district blueprints from a city archive. Witnesses saw her moving along Avenida 9 de Julio beneath a tall white monument.
briefing.callingCard: A torn program from Teatro Colón reads: "Count the lanes, find the needle."

clues: A taxi receipt lists a drop-off at Avenida Corrientes and 9 de Julio.
A witness described a white monument rising from a broad avenue.
A costume tag reads: "Stagehand blacks, Teatro Colón loading door."

puzzle.description: Use the city clues to identify the Buenos Aires landmark Carmen chose as her rendezvous.

options: The Obelisco on Avenida 9 de Julio

briefing.headline: VINTAGE MALBEC VANISHES!
briefing.report: The oldest bottle of Malbec wine in Argentina, dating back to the 19th century, was stolen from a historic winery cellar in Mendoza. The thief left behind coordinate details on an empty bottle label.
briefing.callingCard: A postcard of the Andes with coordinates written in red ink: "32°39'S, 70°00'W"

clues: A winery flyer with a handwritten note: "Deliver grapes to the vineyard worker."
A GPS unit tracking history ending at coordinates 32°39'S, 70°00'W.

briefing.headline: CLOUD TRAIN LOCOMOTIVE LOOTED!
briefing.report: A scale gold model of the Tren a las Nubes locomotive was stolen from the Salta Cathedral museum. The thief was seen fleeing towards the railway station.
briefing.callingCard: A boarding pass stub showing a flight from Buenos Aires and a train connection in Salta.

clues: A jumbled train schedule showing a final stop near the colourful hills of Quebrada de Humahuaca.
A witness statement: "She boarded the train wearing a train conductor's uniform at 08:00."
A souvenir shop bag from the town of Humahuaca with a red hair thread.

puzzle.title: Timeline Reconstruction

puzzle.question: What is the correct chronological sequence and final destination?
options: 08:00 Depart Salta → 11:30 San Antonio de los Cobres → 14:00 La Polvorilla → 16:30 Quebrada de Humahuaca
08:00 Quebrada de Humahuaca → 11:30 La Polvorilla → 14:00 San Antonio → 16:30 Depart Salta
08:00 Depart Salta → 11:30 La Polvorilla → 14:00 Quebrada de Humahuaca → 16:30 San Antonio
08:00 San Antonio → 11:30 Depart Salta → 14:00 La Polvorilla → 16:30 Quebrada de Humahuaca

briefing.headline: HISTORIC LIGHTHOUSE DIAL STOLEN!
briefing.report: The original brass lens rotation gear from the Les Eclaireurs Lighthouse was stolen in the dead of night. The thief escaped by sailboat into the Beagle Channel.
briefing.callingCard: A maritime map grid with compass commands: 'Start at Port. Go East 4, South 2.'

clues: A lighthouse keeper's log noting: "A suspicious woman was asking for the key to the main beacon."
A navigation route map showing a destination at the Les Eclaireurs Lighthouse in the Beagle Channel.
A penguin colony report mentioning a woman in a red coat boarding a boat near the islands.

puzzle.title: Compass Navigation Grid
puzzle.description: Start at Port (Row 1, Col 1). Follow the directions: Go East 4 grid spaces, then South 2 grid spaces. What is your final destination?
puzzle.question: Which landmark coordinate is the final destination?

ushuaia.funFact: Ushuaia is the only city in the world that lies below the 54th parallel south, earning its "End of the World" nickname.
ushuaia.learningObjective: Identify a real Ushuaia landmark or regional feature from connected cultural and geographic clues.
ushuaia.accessibilityDescription: Visual evidence for Compass Navigation Grid in Ushuaia.

briefing.headline: SHIP LOG BOOK STOLEN!
briefing.report: A Beagle Channel ship log vanished before morning departure. Carmen left a red scarf tied to a pier cleat.
briefing.callingCard: A salt-stained card reads: "Follow the channel at the end of the world."

clues: The manifest lists a small boat crossing the Beagle Channel.
A witness saw Carmen wearing a sailor coat.
The route points away from the airport and toward the harbor.

puzzle.title: Harbor Trail
puzzle.description: Use the maritime clues to identify the Ushuaia hideout.
puzzle.question: Where is Carmen going?
puzzle.visualHtml:
⚓

Ship log / harbor cleat

ushuaia.funFact: The Beagle Channel is one of Ushuaia most recognizable maritime settings.
ushuaia.learningObjective: Identify a real Ushuaia landmark or regional feature from connected cultural and geographic clues.

briefing.headline: POSTMARK PLATE PILFERED!
briefing.report: A souvenir postmark plate disappeared from a remote tourism desk. Carmen left a postcard marked "Fin del Mundo."
briefing.callingCard: The card reads: "Send the warrant to the last post."

clues: The clue points to end-of-the-world tourism routes.
A stack of postcards is tied with red thread.
Carmen wore a postal clerk disguise.

puzzle.title: End-of-World Clue
puzzle.description: Use the postcard clues to identify the hideout.
puzzle.question: Where should the warrant team search?
options: End of the World post office
puzzle.visualHtml:
✉

Fin del Mundo

warrantAnswers.hideout: End of the World post office

ushuaia.funFact: Ushuaia is strongly associated with the phrase Fin del Mundo, or End of the World.
ushuaia.learningObjective: Identify a real Ushuaia landmark or regional feature from connected cultural and geographic clues.

briefing.headline: RAIL SPIKE RELIC STOLEN!
briefing.report: A railway relic vanished from a southern heritage display. Carmen left a ticket marked End of the World Train.
briefing.callingCard: The card reads: "Follow the last rails south."

clues: A heritage ticket points to the End of the World Train.
A forest route note mentions Tierra del Fuego landscapes.
The disguise note reads: "Rail guide."

puzzle.title: Southern Rail Clue
puzzle.description: Use the railway and landscape clues to identify the hideout.
puzzle.question: Where did Carmen go?
puzzle.visualHtml: Last rails / southern forest

ushuaia.funFact: Ushuaia is strongly associated with the End of the World identity and Tierra del Fuego landscapes.
ushuaia.learningObjective: Connect place-based clues to a real Argentina location in the ushuaia case pool.
ushuaia.accessibilityDescription: Use the railway and landscape clues to identify the hideout.

briefing.headline: ICE AXE HANDLE MISSING!
briefing.report: A historic mountaineering handle disappeared from a local display. Carmen left crampon marks pointing toward Martial Glacier.
briefing.callingCard: A blue card says: "Climb above the harbor and look back."

clues: A trail note names Martial Glacier.
A witness saw Carmen above the city with a view toward the Beagle Channel.
The disguise tag reads: "Mountain guide."

puzzle.title: Glacier Overlook Match
puzzle.description: Match the mountain and harbor-view clues to the hideout.
puzzle.question: Which hideout matches?
puzzle.visualHtml: Crampon marks / harbor view

ushuaia.funFact: Martial Glacier sits above Ushuaia and is associated with mountain views over the city and Beagle Channel.
ushuaia.learningObjective: Connect place-based clues to a real Argentina location in the ushuaia case pool.
ushuaia.accessibilityDescription: Match the mountain and harbor-view clues to the hideout.

briefing.headline: GLACIER ICE CORE PILFERED!
briefing.report: A scientific ice core sample representing 10,000 years of climate history was stolen from the Glaciarium Museum. The thief left a final note taunting the police.
briefing.callingCard: A letter: "You've chased me far, detective. But my final hideout is locked behind the symbols of my journey."

clues: A tour guide pamphlet: "Walk the Perito Moreno Glacier catwalks."
A glacier trekking harness with red stitching.
A final note mentioning: "Where the ice collapses, the journey ends."

puzzle.title: Final Token Deduction
puzzle.description: Examine the symbols on your collected clue tokens. Identify the 3 locations that contain the Sun, the Ice, and the Wind symbols to locate Carmen's final glacier hideout.
puzzle.question: Which combination of clue tokens contains the Sun, Wind, and Ice symbols?

el-calafate.funFact: The Perito Moreno Glacier is 30km long and 70m tall, and periodically dams the lake before spectacularly collapsing.
el-calafate.learningObjective: Identify a real El Calafate landmark or regional feature from connected cultural and geographic clues.
el-calafate.accessibilityDescription: Visual evidence for Final Token Deduction in El Calafate.

briefing.headline: ICE AGE DIAGRAM TAKEN!
briefing.report: A glacier formation diagram disappeared from a science exhibit. Carmen left a frosted envelope stamped Glaciarium.
briefing.callingCard: A cold card reads: "Learn the ice before you cross it."

clues: A museum ticket is stamped Glaciarium.
The note references glacier science, not a boat dock.
Carmen wore a museum educator badge.

puzzle.title: Science Exhibit Clue
puzzle.description: Use the museum and glacier-science clues.
puzzle.question: Where is Carmen hiding?
puzzle.visualHtml:
❄

Science exhibit / frost envelope

el-calafate.funFact: El Calafate is a gateway for visitors learning about and visiting Patagonian glaciers.
el-calafate.learningObjective: Identify a real El Calafate landmark or regional feature from connected cultural and geographic clues.

briefing.headline: PATAGONIAN ROUTE MAP GONE!
briefing.report: A route map for glacier boats vanished from a travel office. Carmen left a blue ticket stub beside a windblown berry branch.
briefing.callingCard: A card reads: "Where the lake carries ice, I carry the loot."

clues: The boat ticket references Lago Argentino.
The clue mentions ice floating on a large Patagonian lake.
Carmen borrowed a deckhand coat.

puzzle.title: Lake and Ice Trail
puzzle.description: Use the boat-route clues around El Calafate.
puzzle.question: Which hideout fits the evidence?
puzzle.visualHtml:
▰

Lake route / floating ice

el-calafate.funFact: Lago Argentino is the large lake associated with glacier routes near El Calafate.

briefing.headline: LAKE SOUNDING LOG TAKEN!
briefing.report: A boat log from Lago Argentino vanished before a glacier excursion. Carmen left a blue ticket stub near the pier.
briefing.callingCard: The card says: "Follow the largest blue mirror to the ice."

clues: A ferry ticket names Lago Argentino.
A guide mentioned glacier-fed water and floating ice.
The disguise receipt reads: "Boat naturalist."

briefing.headline: CLIMATE CORE LABEL STOLEN!
briefing.report: A museum label for an ice-core exhibit disappeared. Carmen left a chilled glove at the Glaciarium exit.
briefing.callingCard: A frosted card says: "The past is frozen in layers."

clues: A museum wristband is stamped Glaciarium.
The stolen label explains glacier layers and climate history.
Carmen disguised herself as a science educator.
